title: Ribbon like filaments and systems and methods for producing the same
abstract: Various implementations include a melt-spun filament that has a radial cross section that has two substantially parallel linear perimetrical sections and first and second curved perimetrical sections. The first curved perimetrical section extends between first ends of the linear perimetrical sections, and the second curved perimetrical section extends between second ends of the linear perimetrical sections. The curved perimetrical sections are convex. According to some embodiments, the curved perimetrical sections are semi-circular or semi-elliptical. A plurality of melt-spun filaments may be included in a bundle of filaments and/or a yarn made with the melt-spun filaments. In addition, a spinneret plate for spinning the melt-spun filaments and a method of making the melt-spun filaments is also provided.
claims: Claims 1. A melt-spun filament having a radial cross section, the radial cross section having two substantially parallel linear perimetrical sections and first and second curved perimetrical sections, the first curved perimetrical section extending between first ends of the linear perimetrical sections and the second curved perimetrical section extending between second ends of the linear perimetrical sections, wherein the curved perimetrical sections are convex. 2. The melt-spun filament according to claim 1, wherein the radial cross section is capsule shaped. 3. The melt-spun filament according to claim 1, wherein the radial cross section is rice shaped. 4. The melt-spun filament according to claim 3, wherein a plane extends axially through the melt-spun filament, intersects the curved perimetrical ends, and includes a central axis of the filament, and a line that is tangential to one of the ends of the linear perimetrical sections intersects the plane at an angle of between 0Â° and 10Â°. 5. The melt-spun filament according to any of the above claims, further comprising a core and a sheath disposed around the core, the core being formed from a first material, and the sheath being formed from a second material. 6. The melt-spun filament according to claim 5, wherein the first material and the second material are different. 7. The melt-spun filament according to claims 5 or 6, wherein the first material and the second material are selected from the group consisting of polytrimethylene terephthalate (PTT), polyethylene terephthalate (PET), polyamides (PA), and polyolefins. 8. The melt-spun filament according to claim 4, further comprising a first material and a second material, the first material and second material being coupled along the plane, wherein the first material and second material have different coefficients of thermal expansion. 9. The melt-spun filament according to claims 1-3, further comprising a first material and a second material, the first material and second material being coupled along a plane that includes a central axis of the filament, wherein the first material and second material have different coefficients of thermal expansion. 10. The melt-spun filament according to claims 8 or 9, wherein the first material and the second material are selected from the group consisting of polytrimethylene terephthalate (PTT), polyethylene terephthalate (PET), polyamides (PA), and polyolefins. 11. The melt-spun filament according to any of claims 1-6 and 8-9, wherein the filament comprises a thermoplastic material. 12. The melt-spun filament according to any of the above claims, wherein a width to height ratio of the radial cross section is between 3.5:1 and 1.5:1, wherein the width is measured in a direction of the linear perimetrical sections at the widest points of the radial cross section and the height is measured in a direction perpendicular to the width direction. 13. The melt-spun filament according to claim 12, wherein the width to height ratio of the radial cross section is between 3.1 and 2.2:1. 14. The melt-spun filament according to any of the above claims, wherein the curved perimetrical sections are semi-circular. 15. The melt-spun filament according to any of the above claims, wherein the curved perimetrical sections are semi-elliptical. 16. The melt-spun filament according to any of the above claims, wherein the melt-spun filament is converted into a plurality of staple fibers. 17. A spun yarn comprising the staple fibers of claim 16. 18. A bundle of filaments comprising a plurality of melt-spun filaments according to any one of the claims 1 to 15. 19. A filament yarn comprising a plurality of the melt-spun filaments according to any one of claims 1 to 15. 20. A bulked continuous filament yarn comprising a plurality of the melt-spun filaments according to any one of claims 1 to 15. 21. A spinneret plate for producing the melt-spun filament according to claim 1, the spinneret plate comprising one or more capillaries, each capillary having a rectangular shaped outlet opening. 22. A method of making the melt-spun filament according to claim 1 comprising: providing a spinneret plate comprising one or more capillaries, each capillary having a rectangular shaped outlet opening; and feeding at least one melted thermoplastic polymer through the capillary.
